ECE 484 · Principles of Safe Autonomy

F1TENTH Time Trial Challenge:
Reactive Racing Pipeline for Fast and Safe Autonomous Driving

Our goal is to complete a 3-lap autonomous time trial as quickly and reliably as possible. Using onboard LiDAR, reactive free-space reasoning, and an optimized Pure Pursuit controller, we aim to reduce crash penalties and manual rescues while preserving smooth, high-speed navigation.

[Yuanzhe Wang] · [Zhenyu Zhang] · [Murphy Huang]
University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign · Spring 2026 
<div class="stat">
  <strong>3-Lap Goal</strong>
  <span>Finish the time trial with consistent, penalty-free autonomous performance.</span>
</div>
<div class="stat">
  <strong>LiDAR-Driven</strong>
  <span>Use local perception and reactive gap reasoning without hardware modifications.</span>
</div>
<div class="stat">
  <strong>ROS 2 Pipeline</strong>
  <span>Closed-loop data flow from /scan and /odom to Ackermann drive commands.</span>
</div>
<a class="button primary" href="#video">Watch Simulator Demo</a>
<a class="button" href="#architecture">System Block Diagram</a>
<a class="button" href="#team">Task Allocation</a>
  <div>
    <div class="section-tag">Race Strategy</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Project Overview & Approach</h2>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="grid-2">
  <div class="card">
    <p class="section-lead" style="margin-top:0; margin-bottom:14px; max-width:none;">
      To tackle the F1TENTH Time Trial without changing hardware, we focus on a software-first racing stack.
      Our system uses LiDAR-based reactive navigation to identify traversable free space, select safe and efficient local targets,
      and generate smooth steering and speed commands through Pure Pursuit-based control.
    </p>
    <div class="feature-list">
      <div class="feature-item">
        <div class="feature-icon">01</div>
        <div>
          <strong>Fast local perception</strong>
          <span>Continuously process scan data to understand track structure and obstacle proximity in real time.</span>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="feature-item">
        <div class="feature-icon">02</div>
        <div>
          <strong>Gap-aware target selection</strong>
          <span>Choose feasible local goals that balance safety, forward progress, and stability during turns.</span>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="feature-item">
        <div class="feature-icon">03</div>
        <div>
          <strong>Penalty reduction</strong>
          <span>Minimize collisions and rescues by improving robustness in narrow or high-curvature track segments.</span>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="card">
    <div class="placeholder small">
      <div>
        <strong style="font-size:1.1rem;">Add a hero racing image, car render, or track screenshot here</strong>
        <p class="muted">Best choice: one dramatic F1TENTH car image with RViz or track overlay.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="track-line"></div>
    </div>
    <p class="caption">Use this visual area to immediately create an F1 racing identity when the page loads.</p>
  </div>
</div>
  <div>
    <div class="section-tag">Preliminary Demo</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Simulator Validation</h2>
  </div>
</div>
<p class="section-lead">
  We have successfully configured the F1TENTH ROS 2 simulation environment. The video below should demonstrate the initial vehicle setup,
  occupancy grid map, LiDAR scans in RViz2, and the first closed-loop validation of motion and sensing.
</p>
<div class="card">
  <div class="placeholder">
    <div>
      <strong style="font-size:1.15rem;">[Embed your RViz2 / Simulator Demo Video Here]</strong>
      <p class="muted">Replace this box with your screen recording, YouTube iframe, or MP4 preview.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="track-line"></div>
  </div>
  <p class="caption">
    Suggested content: spawn scene, teleop or initial autonomous motion, LiDAR visualization, and track-following behavior.
  </p>
</div>
  <div>
    <div class="section-tag">System Design</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">ROS 2 Closed-Loop Architecture</h2>
  </div>
</div>
<p class="section-lead">
  Our system is organized as a clean closed-loop pipeline: sensor topics provide local scene information, perception modules infer free space,
  goal-generation logic selects an actionable target, and the controller converts this target into Ackermann steering and speed commands.
</p>
<div class="card">
  <div class="pipeline">
    <div class="pipe-box">
      <strong>/scan + /odom</strong>
      <span>LiDAR and vehicle state input</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pipe-box">
      <strong>Preprocessing</strong>
      <span>Filter noise and structure useful local observations</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pipe-box">
      <strong>Gap Detection</strong>
      <span>Identify safe free-space candidates</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pipe-box">
      <strong>Target Generation</strong>
      <span>Select local waypoint or target heading</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pipe-box">
      <strong>Pure Pursuit</strong>
      <span>Compute steering and speed commands</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pipe-box">
      <strong>/drive</strong>
      <span>Ackermann output to simulator or car</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="height:18px"></div>
  <div class="placeholder small">
    <div>
      <strong style="font-size:1.1rem;">[Insert your polished block diagram here]</strong>
      <p class="muted">Keep the arrows and module names consistent with the pipeline above.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <p class="caption">
    Figure 1. End-to-end data flow from local sensing to final control commands, with future deployment onto the Jetson-based physical platform.
  </p>
</div>
  <div>
    <div class="section-tag">Core Logic</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Method Breakdown</h2>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="method-list">
  <div class="mini-card">
    <h3>1. LiDAR Processing</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Filter invalid range values and optionally constrain the field of view.</li>
      <li>Stabilize scan interpretation for local decision-making.</li>
      <li>Prepare structured observations for downstream free-space analysis.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <div class="mini-card">
    <h3>2. Gap Detection</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Detect candidate traversable regions from local scan geometry.</li>
      <li>Measure angular width, clearance, and relative safety of each gap.</li>
      <li>Reject weak candidates that could increase crash risk.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <div class="mini-card">
    <h3>3. Goal Generation</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Select a target direction or local waypoint from the chosen gap.</li>
      <li>Bias the choice toward forward progress and smoother future motion.</li>
      <li>Reduce oscillation through practical heuristics and filtering.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <div class="mini-card">
    <h3>4. Pure Pursuit Control</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Convert local targets into Ackermann steering commands.</li>
      <li>Adjust speed according to curvature, stability, or risk level.</li>
      <li>Maintain smooth closed-loop behavior across straightaways and turns.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="results-note">
  A strong improvement for the final version would be to add one small diagram under each block, such as a LiDAR polar plot, a gap visualization, and a waypoint-to-curvature sketch.
</div>
  <div>
    <div class="section-tag">Execution Plan</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Team Plan & Task Allocation</h2>
  </div>
</div>
<p class="section-lead">
  To move efficiently from simulator validation to real vehicle deployment, the project is divided into tightly coupled technical tracks.
</p>
<div class="allocation-grid">
  <div class="member-card">
    <div class="member-role">Perception</div>
    <h3>[Teammate 1 Name]</h3>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Focus:</strong> LiDAR processing and gap detection</li>
      <li>Filter scan noise and build robust free-space interpretation.</li>
      <li>Implement detection logic for safe, traversable local gaps.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <div class="member-card">
    <div class="member-role">Control</div>
    <h3>[Teammate 2 Name]</h3>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Focus:</strong> Pure Pursuit and speed policy</li>
      <li>Transform local targets into stable steering outputs.</li>
      <li>Tune control behavior for faster and smoother lap performance.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <div class="member-card">
    <div class="member-role">Localization</div>
    <h3>[Teammate 3 Name]</h3>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Focus:</strong> Odometry, TF, and environment understanding</li>
      <li>Maintain correct frame relationships and motion context.</li>
      <li>Support future track-aware improvements if mapping is introduced.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <div class="member-card">
    <div class="member-role">Integration</div>
    <h3>[Teammate 4 Name]</h3>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Focus:</strong> ROS 2 integration and hardware deployment</li>
      <li>Ensure node communication and stable system bring-up.</li>
      <li>Lead the simulator-to-Jetson deployment path for Milestone 3.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</div>
Created for ECE 484: Principles of Safe Autonomy · Styled as a racing-inspired F1TENTH project page.
